authors: Alireza Nadafian, Alireza Mohammadshahi, Majid Yazdani
title: KAPSO: A Knowledge-grounded framework for Autonomous Program Synthesis and Optimization
storage_format: Knowledge system: MediaWiki typed pages (Principle, Implementation, Environment, Heuristic) with a Neo4j graph index + Weaviate vector index, ingesting repos, docs, papers and web content from 2000+ sources. Cognitive memory: episodic store in a Weaviate vector DB with JSON fallback (trigger, lesson, recommended action, provenance for the experiment branch). Experimentation engine: isolated git branches with committed artifacts.
agent_framework: custom (KAPSO: OrchestratorAgent + CodingAgent + SearchStrategy + ContextManager + KnowledgeSearch)
benchmarks_used: MLE-Bench (Kaggle-style ML competitions), ALE-Bench (AtCoder heuristic contests, C++)
performance_metrics: Medal rate (%), Private score, Final Performance (ELO), Rank Percentile (%), Total Cost ($)
baseline_compared: R&D-Agent, AIRA-dojo, ML-Master, AIDE (MLE-Bench); ALE-Agent, ALE Sequential, ALE one-shot (ALE-Bench)
memory_size_over_time: Knowledge base is fixed (pre-built); episodic memory grows with executed experiments; CTIM items bounded by configurable capacity
cost_per_task: ALE-Bench: $914.8 total (11 competitions) ≈ $83/competition; MLE-Bench: $200/task budget, 24h runtime
main_result: MLE-Bench: 50.67% medal rate (vs 35.11% best open-source R&D-Agent, +15.56pp); ALE-Bench: ELO 1909.4, rank percentile 6.1% (vs ALE-Agent 1879.3, 6.8%) at 9% lower cost ($914.8 vs $1003.3).
performance_vs_baseline: +15.56pp medal rate vs R&D-Agent on MLE-Bench; +30.1 ELO vs ALE-Agent on ALE-Bench; advantage concentrated on Hard tasks (+17.78pp)
cost_vs_baseline: ALE-Bench: $914.8 vs $1003.3 ALE-Agent (-8.8%); MLE-Bench: fixed $200 budget does not allow direct comparison
limitations: Limited number of competitions in ALE-Bench introduces noise; variance across tasks; reliance on proprietary LLMs (Gemini); high cost per task; the knowledge package may have selection bias
validity_threats: MLE-Bench and ALE-Bench are ML/algorithm competitions, not traditional SE (bug fixing, code localization); Gemini-2.5-pro as the sole model; knowledge base built by the authors with potentially subjective selection criteria
